node.text: Kam Shing Kwang, CEO for Asia Private Bank and Vice Chair for Greater China Investment Banking, wishes you a prosperous Year of the Rabbit!
New year, new goals: Make 2023 the year you take charge of your personal finances. Tap the link in our bio to explore how you can optimize the path to your goals.
Every smart device you have in your home may put you at risk for cyber threats. Fortify your personal and financial safety with these steps. Tap the link in our bio to learn more.
Today, as we celebrate the life of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., take a moment to consider how we can use his message of freedom, equality and justice to build a better tomorrow for all.

The stock market is anticipating a slowdown in rate hikes, but growth may slow as well. Will the Federal Reserve be able to keep rates higher for longer? #TopMarketTakeaways explores what the latest inflation data may be telling us about these trends. Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

Boom to bust in seven charts. The pandemic and the Federal Reserve’s tightening cycle over the past two years catalyzed sharp macro and market moves, in both directions. COVID-related supply chain disruptions saw the price of a container ship from Shanghai to Los Angeles increase ten-fold before collapsing back. Overexcited investors pushed unprofitable tech and crypto assets toward record highs before settling back down. Most recently, global negative yielding debt completely melted away.
 
Looking ahead, our investment strategists laid out three scenarios that could also surprise us in 2023. In other words, outcomes that are realistic, but aren’t part of our base case. Tap the link in our bio to learn more in last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

What’s staying and what’s changing? This week, we examine why markets outside the United States are off to a better start, and three market scenarios that could arise in 2023. Tap the link in our bio to read today’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

node.text:
Hard to hide. In 2022, persistent inflation, war and an aggressive Federal Reserve pushed stocks and bonds toward one of their worst annual performances on record. Together, a 60/40 portfolio returned -16%, a loss only seen during the Great Financial Crisis. Although we expect a weaker economy in 2023, today’s levels are creating potential opportunities for long-term investors.
 
For more of our thoughts on what we expect this year, tap the link in our bio to check out our Outlook 2023: See the potential—weaker growth, stronger markets.
 
#jpmorganprivatebank #Outlook2023
The affair with the market catalysts of the last decade is over now, and a new era of investing begins. Tap the link in our bio to explore our Eye on the Market Outlook 2023: The End of the Affair.

node.text: We want our clients and colleagues to feel confident in our commitment to diversity. Watch the third video in our series in which our colleagues discuss what Black Wealth Initiatives means to them and the impact it has had on their professional and personal lives.

Discuss the gift of giving at your next family meeting by sharing charitable interests, learning about philanthropic practices and collaborating on a giving plan. Tap the link in our bio to learn more about capturing the spirit of the season through philanthropy.

Mass affluent travelers are bolstering a long-term resurgence in the luxury travel sector. If you’ve yet to consider private investments in travel and leisure real estate, now may be the time. Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

The beginning of the end of the hiking cycle. After the latest inflation data, it seems like we are approaching the end of the Federal Reserve’s hiking cycle. So what happens next? Perhaps surprisingly, stocks and bonds both tend to rally after the Fed’s last rate hike of a cycle, returning more than 20% on average in the two years following the Fed’s final hike. Even as higher interest rates rein in economic growth next year, we think markets could stabilize.
 
For more on our views heading into the new year, tap the link in our bio to read our recently released Outlook 2023: See the potential.
 
#jpmorganprivatebank #Outlook2023

What powerful forces will shape our economy next year? In our #Outlook2023 webcast, we discuss the consequences of global policy tightening, weakness across the global economy and valuation resets. Watch Clay Erwin, Global Head of Investments Sales and Trading, and other market strategy and investment leaders for insights into the year ahead.
 
Tap the link in our bio to watch our Outlook 2023 webcast on demand. #jpmorganprivatebank

Despite volatility and headwinds to growth, we see potential for stronger markets in 2023 and beyond. This week’s #TopMarketTakeaways reviews the key points driving our views on markets and the economy. Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

After one of the worst years for balanced portfolios, investors still have many difficult questions to consider. The bad news: Higher rates will likely rein in growth in 2023. The good news: A dramatic reset in valuations has, in our view, created the most attractive entry point for stocks and bonds in over a decade.
 
For more on these dynamics and the full suite of potential investment opportunities, tap the link in our bio for our newly released Outlook 2023: See the potential.

Just released: Our 2023 Outlook. This year tested the resolve of many investors, as both stocks and bonds suffered losses. In our Outlook, we consider key economic and market forces—higher rates, weaker growth, valuation resets—and discuss what they might mean for investors’ portfolios. 

Tap the link in our bio to explore the #Outlook2023. #jpmorganprivatebank

node.text: What’s next in arts, culture and books? We hope this remarkable collection of experiences opens windows into new opportunities and possibilities. Tap the link in our bio to learn more. #NextList2023

Anticipating quite a bit more market volatility before the end of rate hikes, Private Bank CIO Richard Madigan discusses why investors should remain steadfast as financial conditions tighten, economic activity slows and inflation ultimately moves lower. Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

node.text: Shakespeare and the last three decades of pop music intersect in a coming-of-age story that examines Juliet’s life post-Romeo. Darin Oduyoye, J.P. Morgan Asset & Wealth Management Chief Communications Officer, offers an exclusive look at opening night of “& Juliet,” a must-see theater recommendation on our 2023 NextList.

Our guests previewed one of the most comprehensive exhibits devoted to Sandro Botticelli in the United States—Botticelli and Renaissance Florence: Masterworks from the Uffizi. Charlotte Eyerman, Director and Chief Curator of the JPMorgan Chase Art Collection, led a conversation on art and innovation in Minneapolis and beyond, as well as a private tour of the Private Bank–sponsored exhibit.
 
Tap the link in our bio to learn more about our Private Bank Minneapolis team.

Giving Season is around the corner, which means it’s a good time to reflect on how you are using your portfolio to expand your charitable influence. When taking part in sustainable investing, you can better align your social and environmental goals while also considering your return potential. 

Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

node.text:
At the Private Bank, we’re committed to helping women grow, succeed and thrive in their professional and personal lives. We hosted the second annual PowerHER Austin event to provide attendees with exclusive networking and learning opportunities from like-minded trailblazers. 

Tap the link in our bio to watch the full video.

After a decade of weak returns and limited protection, the bond market has returned to relevance for a larger number of investors. #TopMarketTakeaways explores why fixed income will likely become more valuable as the global rate hiking campaign reaches its final stages.

Tap the link in our bio for more insights.

One week until #ParisPhoto begins!
 
In our exclusive Q&A with photographer, artist and writer @CigHarvey, we sat down to discuss the inspiration behind her work and what to expect at next week’s Paris Photo exhibition.
 
Tap the link in our bio to read the Q&A and learn more about our longstanding ties to @ParisPhotoFair as we celebrate 11 years of partnership.

node.text:
The midterm elections will determine the balance of power in Washington and the direction of policy for the next two years. But after previous midterms, markets historically have had a remarkably consistent pattern no matter the results. Regardless of which party has ended up controlling the Senate and the House of Representatives, equities have tended to rally in the months following the elections. Investors may be worried about what the outcome may mean for markets, but we encourage them to focus on their long-term goals. Staying invested through elections has proven to be the best course of action over time.
 
For more on the potential outcomes and market implications of the 2022 midterm elections, tap the link in our bio to read last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

Cybercriminals can seize control of a phone without having to steal it physically. 

Tap the link in our bio to read more about how phone hijackings happen, and how you can outsmart mobile device hackers in #CybersecurityAwarenessMonth.

With voters set to hit the polls in potentially record numbers, #TopMarketTakeaways breaks down what’s at stake, the most likely outcomes, and why it all matters for portfolios. 

Tap the link in our bio for more insights.

node.text:
We’re thrilled to be returning to Paris next month to mark our 11th year at @ParisPhotoFair. Each year, with different thematic focuses, we’re delighted to shed light on iconic works across a range of emerging and under-represented artists. In 2022, our collection explores the world through a dynamic and distinct dimension in our exhibition, A Different Lens. 

Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

What do your apps know about you? The average smartphone user has between 60 and 90 apps on their device. This #CybersecurityAwarenessMonth, take steps to protect your digital life from cybercriminals. 

Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

History could hold clues into where this market is headed, so in this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways we seek some perspective from the past. One observation: Now may be one of the best entry points on a valuation basis in a decade. 

Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

The third annual Audrain Newport Concours & Motor Week turned the seaside city into a haven for auto enthusiasts where we were proud to be the presenting sponsor of the Tour d’Elegance. A little rain was no match for the outpouring of enthusiasm on display as nearly 100 vintage cars paraded through town.
 
Tap the link in our bio to discover how our Passions & Pursuits Advisory Team can help you plan, invest, borrow and bank to make the most of your passion for rare and collectible automobiles.

Over 80% of people post personal information to their social media accounts every week. Oversharing on your accounts could compromise your personal information. This #CybersecurityAwarenessMonth, learn the precautions you can take now to keep your digital life safe from cyber threats. Tap the link in our bio to explore our insights and best practices to help you become less vulnerable and more cyber secure.
 
Source: Tessian. Data as of February 2021.

Carnegie Hall’s Opening Night Gala helped kick off the fall season with a performance by The Philadelphia Orchestra conducted by Yannick Nézet-Séguin. J.P. Morgan Private Bank was delighted to support a magical evening of arts and entertainment—an integral part of New York City’s culture and economy.

Photo credit: Chris Lee

Art has been an integral part of our history at J.P. Morgan Private Bank, and it’s a passion we share with many of our clients.
 
As we look forward to marking our 11th year as an official partner of @parisphotofair, we continue our longstanding commitment to art and supporting the next generation of emerging and established artists. 
 
Tap the link in our bio to explore some of the highlights of our upcoming 2022 Paris Photo exhibition. #ParisPhoto
 
Photo credit: © Cig Harvey, Courtesy Robert Klein Gallery

node.text:
Higher for longer. A widely anticipated 75-basis-point policy rate hike last week kept the Federal Reserve on track to execute the most aggressive tightening cycle in four decades. At the meeting, Fed Chair Powell made it crystal clear that conquering inflation is proving to be much harder than expected, and the pursuit of that goal is likely to come with collateral economic damage.

For more on our interpretation of what the Fed said and the implications for portfolios, read last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

We were proud to sponsor one of the world's most curated car shows, The Bridge VI. Collectors, enthusiasts and revelers gathered to celebrate the art of the automobile while enjoying tailored hospitality and dramatic scenery. At J.P. Morgan Private Bank, our Passions & Pursuits Advisory Team works with collectors to help optimize their collections for tax efficiency and trust & estate considerations.
 
*JPMorgan Chase & Co., its affiliates, and employees do not provide tax, legal or accounting advice.

node.text:
Another meeting, another hike. The Federal Reserve announced its third consecutive 0.75% policy rate hike at the September FOMC meeting today—keeping the aggressive streak alive in a battle against decades-high inflation. Given persistent economic strength, the market is pricing in an additional 1% of hikes through early next year. However, one segment of the economy is feeling the intended effects of Fed policy: the housing market.
 
We continue to see signs of deteriorating demand. New home sales saw their most significant year-to-date fall on record, and the traffic of prospective home buyers collapsed to its lowest level in over 10 years. The bottom line: “Bad news” can be good news for the Fed. Slowing growth may signal lower inflation ahead, which may allow the central bank to take its foot off the brakes in time to extend this cycle.
 
For a reminder on reasons to stay invested and potential opportunities to capitalize on when markets are volatile, tap the link in our bio to read last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

node.text:
How do you spell relief? How about over 80 straight days of declining prices at the pump? U.S. retail gasoline prices are over 40% lower than their sweltering highs reached in June. The benefits of falling gasoline prices are clear. The U.S. consumes almost 380 million gallons of gasoline per day. That means the price drop from over $5 per gallon to $3.78 today equates to almost $470 million of daily savings. It also means lower airline fares (down almost 10% from their peak in May), lower long-term consumer inflation expectations (back below 3% for the first time this year), and more money to spend elsewhere (recreational goods spending was the quickest since January last month).

While Europe is facing a daunting energy crisis, U.S. consumers are probably feeling a little bit better about the world than they were earlier this summer. 

For a full recap of this past summer and our latest thinking, tap the link in our bio to check out last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

For years, planning to spend 4% of your assets each year in retirement was a generally accepted guidepost. But these days, following “the 4% rule” could lead to an early drain of financial resources—even for those with great fortunes. So how do you determine what you can safely spend? The answer depends on your situation, but we’ve outlined some factors everyone can consider. 

Tap the link in our bio for the full article.

Jamie Dimon and members of the Operating Committee took to the road earlier this month for their annual summer bus tour. To commemorate the one-year anniversary of the Private Bank’s Black Wealth Initiatives program, the team made a stop on Martha’s Vineyard to join BWI Bankers and other senior leaders across the firm for a special evening. Jamie led a memorable fireside chat to discuss the firm’s commitment to driving inclusive economic growth around the world.

The Federal Reserve wants to see growth slow in order to bring down inflation, but is there a trigger in the data that would cause it to slow rate hikes or pause? Despite weak numbers from the housing sector, a slight rise in initial jobless claims and poor sentiment-based measures, there isn’t much of a signal that growth is slowing markedly. In fact, economic surprises in every sector but housing and surveys have been positive recently. 

For more insights, tap the link in our bio to check out this week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

The beginning of a school year can be a critical time for your family to reinforce values, set expectations and enable deliberate decision making. Equip your student with tools and strategies so they can make smart academic, social and financial decisions this school year and beyond. Tap the link in bio for more insights.

We’re proud to sponsor the Allied Marine Private Rendezvous in Montauk. We understand the sense of tranquility that comes from being on the open seas. 

Tap the link in our bio to discover how our Passions & Pursuits Team at J.P. Morgan Private Bank can help you plan, invest, borrow and bank to make the most of your passions.

The landmark bill signed into law this week was focused on climate, healthcare and corporate taxes, but it likely will have little effect on inflation. The biggest impact looks to be on long-term sustainability goals, solidifying our focus on themes such as renewable energy, battery storage, green real estate and clean infrastructure.

Tap link in bio for more insights from #TopMarketTakeaways

Even in dislocated markets, it's important to know how investors can make the most of their investments. Fixed income offers three components that may help minimize risk and achieve equity-like returns in portfolios. 

Tap the link in our bio for more insights.

We recently partnered with Chase Auto and McLaren Americas to support the launch event of the new McLaren Artura, the storied automaker’s newest supercar. Guests were treated to an evening of luxury hospitality and an early glimpse of the highly anticipated hybrid with its beautiful lines and all-new carbon fiber architecture. 

Tap the link in our bio to find out more about how we can help put your passions in overdrive.

A refreshing bit of good news. July Consumer Price Index (CPI) data was released this week, showing that the peak of decades-high inflation may be in the rearview. The headline print ran at a year-over-year pace of +8.5%, a nudge lower from +9.1% in June, and ex-volatile food and energy rose +5.9% year-over-year. More importantly, prices didn’t rise at all in July relative to June, and we take that as a welcome sign. Markets are also celebrating the news, given that the Federal Reserve may have a window to ease up in its fight against inflation. The slowdown in inflation is encouraging, even if the coast isn’t clear yet.
 
For more on how to navigate a highly uncertain environment, tap the link in our bio to check out last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.

How can entrepreneurs position themselves for success in an NFT-driven world that is poised to change fashion, sports, fine art, social justice and more? Marc Beckman, Founder of the NFT digital artwork platform Truesy, guides us through the fundamentals of non-fungible tokens, digital artwork and blockchain technology.
 
Explore our full Summer Reading List by tapping the link in our bio. #SRL2022

While the recent stock market rally is certainly welcome, it’s important for investors to consider what’s happened in similar situations in the past. Stocks may be rebounding right now, but in an atmosphere this rife with uncertainty, investors may want to seek out opportunities that could allow them to stay on track toward their goals while taking less risk.
 
Tap the link in our bio for more from #TopMarketTakeaways

Conversations about finances can be difficult and sometimes emotional—and financial compatibility between two partners is rarely achieved without exploring what money means to each of you. If starting a family is important, then long-lasting financial considerations will likely follow. Or, if you’re already married, anticipating the future by creating longer-term goals can provide clarity, purpose and a sense of security. Finally, re-evaluating your lifestyle choices and considering your lifestyle can allow a seamless transition to retirement. 

Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

Well, that escalated quickly. As Federal Reserve Chair Powell put it: “Stable prices are the bedrock of the economy.” And prices this year have been anything but that. If you asked investors at the start of the year what the Fed’s policy rate would be at the halfway mark, it’s a safe bet that 2.50% (and rising!) would not have been the answer. On Wednesday, the central bank raised interest rates by a unanimous 75bps, making it clear that they are laser-focused on bending inflation back to their 2% long-run target. In our view, a slowing economy is what is likely needed to bring inflation down, opening the door for welcome policy easing at the end of this year. 

For more insights, tap the link in our bio to check out our recent #TopMarketTakeaways.

Whether or not we’re in a recession, the markets seem to have been breathing a sigh of relief. We think investors are better off focusing on the more important signals that came from the GDP release and the Fed’s meeting on Wednesday. 

Tap the link in our bio for more from #TopMarketTakeaways

When thinking about passing wealth on to children and grandchildren, it’s wise to contemplate when it might be best to make that gift—during lifetime or afterward? This can be a difficult decision. To be sure, the economics of giving may be at their best (during life) when the psychology of giving may be at its worst. But this dynamic only underscores how important it is to explore both the quantitative (is it affordable?) and qualitative (is it wise?) sides of all potential gifts. 

Tap the link in our bio to learn more from our Wealth Transfer Guide.

Europe is facing a perfect storm of unbridled uncertainty, with a trio of dilemmas that could drive the region into recession: an energy shortage, impending policy tightening and political upheaval in both Italy and the U.K. But despite all this, the European investment landscape is evolving, and new areas of opportunity are emerging—especially for longer-term investors.

Tap the link in our bio for more from #TopMarketTakeaways

node.text:
Consumer perception appears far from historical reality. While the June inflation report has dominated headlines this week, it is important for investors to maintain a longer-run mindset. The outlook may seem bleak, but the data shows that investors tend to be overly pessimistic about the future of stocks. The New York Federal Reserve conducts an ongoing survey of U.S. consumers that asks if they expect stock prices to be higher a year from now. On average, over the past eight years, only four out of every 10 respondents have thought so. However, the reality—based on historical S&P 500 returns starting from 1950—is that the likelihood of achieving a positive return one year later is 80%, roughly double that of consumer perception. Blind optimism can hurt portfolio performance in the short term, but allowing pessimism to derail your long-term plan can be even more detrimental to reaching your goals.

For more on how investors can invest during volatility, tap the link in our bio to explore our #MidYearOutlook.

Amid inflation, war in Ukraine and the fallout from lockdowns in China, the outlook for economies and markets has become more challenged. But with tailwinds coming from healthy consumers, corporations and innovation, we see paths to help you stay invested and strengthen your portfolio.

Tap the link in bio to download our #MidYearOutlook.

The Federal Reserve's message seems clear: Its focus is on containing inflation, even at the expense of growth. And many investors believe a recession is now more likely in the cards. What to make of it all? In #TopMarketTakeaways, we highlight five observations on a critical week for the markets.
 
Tap the link in our bio to learn more.

Just released: Mid-Year Outlook 2022—Hurricane or storm clouds: A world in transition. Challenges have emerged for investors looking to navigate this business cycle and the next: The era of easy money is winding down, the war in Ukraine has strained commodity supplies, and lockdowns in China have threatened global growth. Will these risks swirl together to form an economic hurricane, or will it all be a passing storm?

node.text:
How high can gasoline prices rise before consumers start to change their behaviors? Likely higher with summer travel season ahead. We examine the forces driving prices and the implications for investors.

Tap the link in bio for our #TopMarketTakeaways

*Investment products and strategies may not be appropriate for all investors.

It was a weekend to remember for automotive enthusiasts who were dazzled by some the world’s finest vehicles at the Greenwich Concours d’Elegance. The Private Bank was excited to be an event sponsor and get an up-close look at the rare automobiles on display.
 
Tap the link in our bio to discover how our Passions & Pursuits Advisory Team can help you make the most of your interests, build your collection and help make sure it can last for generations to come.
 
Photo Credit: Deremer Studios

With the disruption of the pandemic, more clients are considering new careers or business opportunities, moving up retirement dates and altering timelines for selling their companies. While each person’s situation, priorities and numbers are unique, the process is the same. It requires looking at your complete financial picture and projecting your wealth’s potential under a variety of investment choices and market conditions. 

Tap the link in our bio to learn how we can help you find the path that’s right for you.
 
Source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, Haver Analytics. Data as of March 2022.

With investors keeping a close eye on economic data to get a sense of how the Fed may be leaning, there’s plenty of fodder to make both bullish and bearish cases for markets. To help guide our investment approach, we unpack what we see happening now in oil, bonds and equities.
 
Tap the link in our bio to explore more insights from #TopMarketTakeaways

node.text:
The interest-rate-sensitive housing market may be a harbinger for the broader economy. Mortgage rates have surged higher amid the Fed’s aggressive tightening campaign to fight inflation. Between that and the appreciation of home values, the monthly cost of owning a house has surged over the past year. As a result, housing activity like home sales has moved sharply lower. That’s the Fed’s goal: Slow activity down, so that inflation follows.
 
For more on where we stand with inflation, tap the link in our bio to check out last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.
 
Chart 1 Source: Redfin, Bankrate.com, Haver Analytics, Bloomberg Finance L.P. Data as of April 30, 2022. *Monthly mortgage payment assumes a 20% down payment on price of median U.S. single family home and the national average 30-year fixed mortgage rate at the time specified.
 
Chart 2 Source: National Association of Realtors, Bloomberg Finance L.P. Data as of April 30, 2022.

Investors are paying increasing attention to how ESG factors affect corporate performance. We zero in on one critical factor, human capital management—the skills, knowledge and experience employees bring to an organization and the value they create. Done right, a focus on ESG issues, including human capital, can potentially lower investment risk and generate higher returns. 

Tap the link in our bio to read more.

We believe inflation appears set to decelerate through year-end, but we’re at delicate place when it comes to the Fed’s path forward. For perspective, we look at the inflation picture from three key areas: the labor market, good vs. services, and housing.
 
Tap the link in our bio to explore more insights in #TopMarketTakeaways.

node.text:
Sentiment remains poor. Uncertainty emanating from elevated inflation, continued lockdowns in Asia and a global commodity supply crunch are plaguing the economic landscape. As such, it’s perhaps unsurprising that the UMichigan Consumer Sentiment Survey is at the lowest level since 2011. That said, history suggests that periods marked by this much pessimism may actually present compelling market entry points for long-term investors. Looking back at the last four times the index reached levels comparable to today, the S&P 500 returned an average of +20% over the next 12 months.
 
For more on recent developments and how they affect the team’s investment decisions, tap the link in our bio to read last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.
 
Source: Bloomberg Finance L.P. Data as of May 17, 2022.

node.text:
Volatility continues to be the word of 2022. This year marks the fourth-worst start of the year* for the S&P 500 on record, with a maximum drawdown of -17.8%, as persistent inflation and the Federal Reserve’s response are causing investors’ concern. But with volatility comes potential opportunity, and this market is presenting some of the most compelling entry points in several years for long-term investors.

To find out where we see opportunity, tap the link in our bio to read our latest insights in #TopMarketTakeaways.

Source: Bloomberg Finance L.P. Data as of May 18, 2022. *Worst start of the year is defined as the largest drawdown from January 1 to May 18 of each respective year.

As part of our commitment to our local community, we’re proud to support the Central Park Conservancy’s Frederick Law Olmsted Awards Luncheon, fondly known as the “Hat Lunch.” A celebration of Central Park, this annual event highlights the role the public can play in the maintenance and care of the park. Just over $4 million was raised this year with several hundred guests in attendance. JPMorgan Chase & Co., represented by Nelle P. Miller, Co-Head of New York at the Private Bank, and former Women’s Committee President Alexia Leuschen were honored for their exemplary generosity and leadership.
 
Photo Credit: Julie Skarratt

Michael Cembalest, Chairman of Market & Investment Strategy, released his 12th annual energy paper which dives into the present energy landscape and takes a look at the challenges posed by the transition to a deeply decarbonized world, geopolitical uncertainty, commodities volatility and energy independence. 

The paper sheds light on the complexities of energy to identify where to deploy capital, and on which new technologies are best positioned to meaningfully decarbonize energy consumption in the years ahead. 

Tap the link in our bio to explore more.

Success Academy Charter Schools has worked to address the educational needs of some of New York City’s most vulnerable families and kids for 15 years. JPMorgan Chase has been a proud supporter. We were pleased to witness Michael Bloomberg’s and Bloomberg Philanthropies’ incredible generosity in support of Success, which has announced plans to build a transformational K–12 campus in the South Bronx with this monumental gift of $100 million. The Private Bank’s own Lorenzo Smith III (Chair of Success Academy NYC) and some of our colleagues had the privilege of visiting with an incredibly impressive group of SA alums on this special night.

It's tempting to move to cash during times of market volatility, but staying invested has historically proven to be a better approach for long-term investors. 

Tap the link in bio for more insights from #TopMarketTakeaways

Source: Bloomberg Finance L.P. Data as of May 12, 2022.
 
*Past performance is no guarantee of future results.

node.text:
The Federal Reserve is continuing its journey to slow growth and inflation. Last week, they announced a 50bps interest rate hike—double the size of the first 25bps hike delivered in March. Some market watchers have pointed to the 1994–1995 hiking cycle as an analog, which consisted of seven interest rate hikes in just over a year (Chart 1). A key difference between then and now is that inflation is materially higher today than in 1994 (Chart 2). The Punchline: The likelihood the Fed tames inflation without unnerving the equity markets looks difficult, but possible (Chart 3).
 
For more insights on the journey of the Fed and the recent market volatility, tap the link our bio to check out last week’s #TopMarketTakeaways.
 
Sources: [1] Bloomberg Finance L.P. Data as of May 11, 2022 [2] Bureau of Economic Statistics, Haver Analytics. Data as of April 30, 2022. [3] Bloomberg Finance L.P. Data as of May 11, 2022. Note: Data for both hiking cycles begins one month prior to first hike.  
 
*Past performance is no guarantee of future results. You cannot invest directly in an index.

How do you empower women to achieve success beyond the playing field? J.P. Morgan convened Los Angeles’ leading female executives in sports to tackle this question. During a roundtable on the sidelines of the 2022 Women’s Leadership Summit, we addressed women’s entrepreneurship in the business of sport, smart DE&I strategies, and engaging the next generation of athletes and fans with an innovative approach.

Thank you Euromoney for recognizing us as the World’s Best Private Bank and for 200 more awards! Our teams in the United States and internationally united in London to accept these accolades. At J.P. Morgan Private Bank, we’re committed to helping our clients and their families experience all the possibilities their wealth can create. Congratulations to all of our colleagues far and wide for consistently raising the standard in Private Banking.

🏆#JPMorganPrivateBank #DreamTeam

*Awards or rankings are not indicative of future success or results.
